page_content:
Prerequisite: PHYS613 or PHYS623.

PHYS625 Quantum Many-Body Theory | (3 Credits)

Non-relativistic second quantization, path integrals and functional field integrals, perturbation theory with Feynman diagrams, interacting electron gas.

Prerequisite: PHYS612 and PHYS613.

PHYS626 Quantum Many-Body Theory II (3 Credits)

Continuation of PHYS625. Functional-integral formulation of quantum field theory. Phase transitions and broken symmetry. Magnetism, superconductivity, and other applications. The renormalization group. Prerequisite: PHYS612, PHYS613, and PHYS625.
